statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a string of unique characters and a boolean value to indicate if the characters are case sensitive.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the number of nodes in the trie.

hints.text: First hint text: Consider the case where the input string has duplicate characters.
Second hint text: Think about the case when the input string has no characters.

editorial.title: Trie Node Count
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: The optimal solution is to iterate through the input string and for each character, check if it already exists in the trie. If it exists, increment the count. Otherwise, add the character to the trie and continue.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Example run 1, Example run 2.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not handling edge cases properly.
Mistake 2: Not considering the case when the input string is empty.

functionSignature.python: def countNodes(self, s: str, caseSensitive: bool) -> int:
